interview.text: Can you walk me through the architecture of the predictive model you developed at Tech Innovations Inc. and explain why you chose that specific approach?
I used a machine learning model because it seemed good for prediction.
How would you design a simple REST API using Flask or FastAPI to serve predictions from a pre-trained scikit-learn model?
I would make an endpoint and call the model from it.
Describe a time you had to clean a messy dataset. What specific pandas or NumPy functions did you use to handle missing values or outliers?
I cleaned the data with pandas and removed bad rows.
If you were tasked with deploying a model prototype, what are the first three steps you would take to ensure it is reproducible and scalable?
I would put it online and test that it works.
Tell me about a time you had to explain a complex technical finding to a non-technical stakeholder. How did you ensure they understood the impact?
I explained the model in simple words and they understood.
How do you manage version control when working on a team project? Can you explain the difference between a 'git merge' and a 'git rebase'?
I use git to save code, and merge and rebase are both ways to update code.
When evaluating a classification model, why might accuracy be a misleading metric, and what other metrics would you use for an imbalanced dataset?
Accuracy can be wrong sometimes, so I would also look at other scores.
If you were asked to research a new AI technique or paper, what is your process for summarizing it and determining if it is practical for your current project?
I would read it and write down the main ideas.
Describe a situation where you disagreed with a teammate's technical approach. How did you resolve the conflict?
We talked about it and chose the better idea.
Given a large dataset, how do you decide which features are most important for your model, and what tools do you use to visualize these relationships?
I check the columns and use graphs to see what matters.
